narrative:
Alisson Becker, representing Brazil, is an elite goalkeeper at this tournament. His overall rating stands at 7.57/10, placing him in the top 2% of goalkeepers. This rating reflects both his club form over the last two seasons and his current World Cup performances.

At this World Cup, Alisson averages 3.333 saves per 90 minutes, showcasing his critical shot-stopping ability. He also contributes 1.0 clearances per 90 minutes, indicating his active involvement in defensive actions beyond his line. His expected assists per 90 minutes is 0.003, which is typical for a goalkeeper and lower than his club average of 0.009.

Alisson is a cornerstone for Brazil, with 38% of his strong rating derived from his play in this World Cup. Scouts should continue to watch for his consistent save-making and his overall command of the penalty area. Fans can expect him to maintain his high standard as Brazil progresses deeper into the tournament.
